title: Neyraudia madagascariensis
taxonomy: Neyraudia madagascariensis is classified as a genus and species of grass within the family Poaceae. Specifically, it belongs to the tribe Arundineae and is closely related to other species in the same genus. The genus Neyraudia is a part of the subfamily Bambusoideae, which includes various bamboo-like plants. Neyraudia madagascariensis is native to Madagascar and is considered an endemic species. Its classification as a plant species is further supported by its unique morphological characteristics and genetic makeup.
